summary: Enamel can't repair itself due to a lack of odontoblastic cells after development; it also doesn’t have an inherent regenerative mechanism owing to minimal organic content and no blood vessels supplying nutrients needed for healing. Thus, options focusing on cell disappearance and low organic composition support this conclusion effectively. Answer: \boxed{\text{Enamel lacks developing cells and essential nutrients}}.
